theme: Fix + Wire up what is already built
context: All infrastructure from v1.5.0 is in place — HMAC auth, 35-feature ML, Railway deployed, /train endpoint exists. This release wires it all to the UI and hardens the foundation.

tasks.title: Fix /stats test assertion
tasks.description: GET /stats returns HTTP 200 but the test checks for wrong response keys, causing a false failure. Update the assertion in test_local.py to match the actual response structure.

tasks.title: SSE Streaming in VS Code Panel
tasks.description: Wire the existing /extension/analyze/stream endpoint (Server-Sent Events) to the panel UI. Replace the current blocking call to /extension/analyze with a streaming connection that shows progressive confidence updates: pattern_match (20%) → entropy (40%) → context (65%) → llm_analysis (85%) → final (100%).
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Panel shows animated progress bar while analysis streams in 5 stages

tasks.title: Wire User Feedback to /train Endpoint
tasks.description: The /train endpoint already exists on Railway. Add 'Confirm Secret' and 'Mark as False Positive' buttons to the panel secrets list. On click, POST to /train with the secret value, context, variable name, label, and user_action. This enables continuous learning from real usage.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Confirm Secret' sends a POST /train request with correct payload and the model loss decreases over time

tasks.title: Persist Trained Model Across Railway Deploys
tasks.description: Currently the ML model weights (llm_model.json) are lost on every Railway redeploy because the container filesystem is ephemeral. Mount a Railway persistent volume at /data/ and save/load the model file from there. Fallback to re-initializing if volume is empty.
implementation_options: Save model as BYTEA in PostgreSQL
Commit model file to git (not recommended — binary bloat)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Model trained before deploy survives a Railway redeploy and test results remain the same

tasks.title: Upgrade Major Dependencies
tasks.description: Upgrade all major dependencies that were held back in v1.5.0 due to breaking change risk. Test thoroughly after each upgrade.

upgrades.risk: must migrate .eslintrc.json to eslint.config.js flat config
must upgrade in sync with eslint

tasks.acceptance_criteria: npm run compile passes with 0 errors, npm audit shows 0 vulnerabilities

tasks.title: Implement Proper Backpropagation in ML Model
tasks.description: The current _simple_gradient_update() only updates the final classifier layer bias and weights. The attention mechanism and FFN layers never learn. Implement real backpropagation through all layers using chain rule. Add Adam optimizer with learning rate scheduling.
tasks.technical_notes: Consider using PyTorch autograd instead of manual backprop to avoid numerical instability
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Training loss decreases consistently across 10+ epochs. Non-secret strings correctly classified as low confidence after 50+ training samples.

tasks.title: Expand Training Dataset to 150+ Samples
tasks.description: Current training set has 30 samples (16 high, 4 medium, 10 false positive). Expand to 150+ with better class balance. Add new secret types not currently covered.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All 3 previously failing test cases (Stripe, GitHub, plain string) pass with correct confidence

tasks.title: Update CHANGELOG and Publish v1.6.0
tasks.description: Document all v1.6.0 changes in CHANGELOG.md, bump version in package.json to 1.6.0, run vsce publish.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Extension v1.6.0 visible on VS Code Marketplace

priority_guide.P1: Should have — core value of this release
priority_guide.P2: Nice to have — include if time permits

success_criteria: Panel shows live streaming confidence updates
User feedback reaches /train endpoint and model loss decreases
Training loss decreases consistently across epochs
depends_on: v1.5.0 — HMAC auth + 35-feature ML + Railway deployment
